body.title: CLOSED: Wire CC Stop hook to ChatLogHandler; retire chat_log_formatter.py
body.result: cc_log_stop_hook.py ships: searches all CC project dirs by mtime, ingests via ChatLogHandler.flush(); chat_log_formatter.py deleted; settings.json Stop hook updated. commit 8a097f2c
body.description:
Stop hook currently calls chat_log_formatter.py (incremental, incompatible format with export_chat.py). Both bespoke scripts should be replaced once T-cc-log-handler-class ships the canonical ChatLogHandler.

Proposed shape: new lab/claudecode/cc_log_stop_hook.py — thin driver that instantiates ChatLogHandler, reads newest session JSONL from ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-TheIgors/, and processes new entries since last run (same cursor-tracking as chat_log_formatter.py). Update ~/.claude/settings.json Stop hook to call this instead of chat_log_formatter.py. Retire chat_log_formatter.py (delete or archive). The /export-chat slash command continues to call export_chat.py for manual full-rebuild use.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_log_stop_hook.py (new), ~/.claude/settings.json (hook update), lab/claudecode/chat_log_formatter.py (delete)
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** wiring only — no new rendering logic. All logic in T-cc-log-handler-class. export_chat.py stays as-is for manual use.
**Test plan:** run cc_log_stop_hook.py manually after a session turn; verify ~/.agent_datacenter/logs/CC.0/YYYY-MM-DD.md updates with correct format. Confirm chat_log_formatter.py is gone from settings.json.
**Pass condition:** Stop hook updates the CC.0 day log in ### User / ### Assistant format after each turn; usage-cache hook still fires (second hook unblocked).
